data.name: bandedGridColumn27.ToolTip
data.value: This will remove any non-printable characters. Non-printable characters are parts of a character set that do not represent a written symbol or part of the text within a document or code, but rather are there in the context of signal and control in character encoding. Non-printable characters are used to indicate certain formatting actions, such as: White spaces (considered an invisible graphic), Carriage returns, Tabs, Line breaks, Page breaks and Null characters.

data.name: bandedGridColumn24.ToolTip
data.value: Join values (must be text values) from multiple columns and to place them into a newly created column. For example, on a list that contains the fields "Firstname" and 'Surname" with the values "John" and "Smith", this may be concatenated to give "John Smith" in a seperate column.

data.name: gridColumn36.ToolTip
data.value: Number of records that contain non-printable characters. Non-printable characters are parts of a character set that do not represent a written symbol or part of the text within a document or code, but rather are there in the context of signal and control in character encoding. Non-printable characters are used to indicate certain formatting actions, such as: White spaces (considered an invisible graphic), Carriage returns, Tabs, Line breaks, Page breaks and Null characters

data.name: gridColumn14.ToolTip
data.value: Number of records that contain punctuation marks. Punctuation marks are: period, comma, question mark, hyphen, dash, parentheses, apostrophe, ellipsis, quotation mark, colon, semicolon, exclamation point.
